metadata.goal: Automate Proxmox VM provisioning, Talos install, and K8s cluster configuration (MetalLB, cert-manager with own CA, Longhorn) for a small cluster (1 CP + 1 worker, expandable).

features.description: Extend nix flake with OpenTofu, yq, jq; keep existing tools and devShell banner.
acceptance: `nix develop` enters the shell and prints the existing 'Atricore k8s2 #4' banner without errors.
`tofu version` reports OpenTofu >= 1.7 inside the devShell.
`yq --version` and `jq --version` succeed inside the devShell.
All previously-listed tools remain in PATH: kubectl, kubecolor, kustomize, kubeseal, helm, k9s, openssl, talosctl, cmctl, just, envsubst, argo, crane.
Existing devShell exports remain intact: A3C_HOME, CP_IP, WK0_IP, KUBECONFIG, TALOSCONFIG.
flake.lock is regenerated and committed.

features.description: Establish .env scheme, secrets/ layout, .gitignore, and Justfile skeleton with init-config and env-check recipes.
acceptance: `.env.example` exists at repo root with all variables documented inline (Proxmox, cluster identity, nodes, network, VM sizing, Talos version, MetalLB range, CA paths).
`Justfile` exists with `set dotenv-load` and recipes: `default` (lists recipes), `init-config`, `env-check`.

`just init-config` on a clean checkout (no .env) creates `.env` from `.env.example` and prompts for required-empty values (PROXMOX_API_TOKEN_SECRET at minimum); idempotent (re-running with .env present is a no-op or only prompts for new vars).
`just env-check` exits 0 when every variable in `.env.example` is set and non-empty in `.env`; exits 1 with a clear message naming any missing vars.
`secrets/` directory exists with `.gitkeep` and `README.md` (explains the directory holds CA material and other gitignored secrets).

`.gitignore` excludes: `.env`, `secrets/*` (with `!secrets/.gitkeep` and `!secrets/README.md` exceptions), `_out/`, `.terraform/`, `.terraform.lock.hcl`, `*.tfstate*`, `*.tfvars` (with `!*.tfvars.example` exception).
Existing `.gitignore` entries (kubeconfig, talosconfig, tmp, jpkl) are preserved.

features.description: Generate a Talos Image Factory schematic with qemu-guest-agent + iscsi-tools + util-linux-tools, download the matching ISO, and upload it to Proxmox ISO storage.
acceptance: `just talos-image` runs end-to-end and is idempotent.
Schematic JSON includes exactly these system extensions: `siderolabs/qemu-guest-agent`, `siderolabs/iscsi-tools`, `siderolabs/util-linux-tools`.
Schematic ID is POSTed to `https://factory.talos.dev/schematics` and the returned id is persisted to `_out/talos-schematic-id`.
ISO is downloaded from `https://factory.talos.dev/image/<schematic-id>/<TALOS_VERSION>/metal-amd64.iso` to `_out/talos-<TALOS_VERSION>-<schematic-id-short>.iso`.
ISO is uploaded to Proxmox storage `${PROXMOX_ISO_STORAGE}` on node `${PROXMOX_NODE}` via the Proxmox API; subsequent runs detect existing remote ISO and skip upload.
Recipe fails fast with a clear error if `just env-check` would fail.

features.description: OpenTofu module provisioning CP + WK0 VMs on Proxmox via bpg/proxmox provider, parameterized by .env-derived tfvars.
acceptance: `infra/` directory contains: `providers.tf`, `variables.tf`, `main.tf`, `outputs.tf`, `cluster.tfvars.example`, `cluster.tfvars.tpl`.
Provider is `bpg/proxmox` pinned to a known-good version (>= 0.66, < 1.0); `tofu init` succeeds.
`just infra-render` produces `infra/cluster.tfvars` from `.env` via envsubst, populating proxmox endpoint, token, node, storage pool, ISO file id, and per-VM specs.
`just infra-up` runs `tofu apply -auto-approve -var-file=cluster.tfvars` and creates exactly two VMs on `${PROXMOX_NODE}`: cp-${CLUSTER_NAME} and wk0-${CLUSTER_NAME}.

Each VM has: configured cores (`VM_CORES`), memory (`VM_MEMORY_MB`), one disk on `${PROXMOX_STORAGE_POOL}` of size `VM_DISK_SIZE_GB` GiB, NIC on bridge `${NETWORK_BRIDGE}` with no VLAN tag, ISO mounted as cdrom from the file uploaded by `talos.image-factory-schematic`.
Each VM has boot order set so it boots from the cdrom (Talos ISO) on first boot, then from disk after install; VMs are powered on after apply.
`just infra-down` runs `tofu destroy -auto-approve -var-file=cluster.tfvars` and removes both VMs cleanly.
Re-running `just infra-up` against an existing apply is a no-op (`tofu plan` shows no changes).
Outputs expose VM ids and configured IPs for downstream features.

features.description: Generate Talos controlplane and worker machine configs with per-node patches (hostname, static IP, gateway, DNS, install disk) under _out/.
acceptance: `just talos-config` runs `talosctl gen config ${CLUSTER_NAME} https://${CP_IP}:6443 --output-dir _out` once; reuses existing `_out/secrets.yaml` on subsequent runs (does not regenerate cluster PKI).

Per-node patches written to `_out/patches/cp.yaml` and `_out/patches/wk0.yaml`, each setting: `machine.network.hostname`, `machine.network.interfaces[0]` (deviceSelector by physical:true), addresses (`<IP>/<NETWORK_CIDR>`), default route via `${NETWORK_GATEWAY}`, `machine.network.nameservers` = [`${NETWORK_DNS}`], `machine.install.disk` = `/dev/sda`.
Final per-node configs at `_out/cp.yaml` and `_out/wk0.yaml` produced via `talosctl machineconfig patch <base> --patch @_out/patches/<node>.yaml`.
`talosctl validate --config _out/cp.yaml --mode metal` and `--config _out/wk0.yaml --mode metal` both succeed.
Generated `_out/talosconfig` is patched with endpoints = [`${CP_IP}`] and nodes = [`${CP_IP}`, `${WK0_IP}`].
Re-running `just talos-config` after editing `.env` regenerates patches and final configs but does NOT regenerate the cluster secrets bundle (`_out/secrets.yaml`).

features.description: Apply machine configs to both VMs, bootstrap etcd on the control plane, and fetch kubeconfig + talosconfig.
acceptance: `just talos-apply` runs `talosctl apply-config --insecure -n ${CP_IP} -f _out/cp.yaml` and `talosctl apply-config --insecure -n ${WK0_IP} -f _out/wk0.yaml`; both succeed.
`just talos-bootstrap` runs `talosctl bootstrap -n ${CP_IP} --talosconfig _out/talosconfig` exactly once; subsequent invocations detect bootstrap already done and exit 0 with a notice (no error).
`just kubeconfig` writes a valid kubeconfig to `${KUBECONFIG}` (i.e. `_out/kubeconfig` per flake) via `talosctl kubeconfig --talosconfig _out/talosconfig -n ${CP_IP} ${KUBECONFIG} --force`.
After bootstrap, `kubectl get nodes` lists exactly two nodes (`${CP_HOSTNAME}` and `${WK0_HOSTNAME}`) both in `Ready` state within 5 minutes.
`kubectl get pods -A` shows kube-system pods (kube-apiserver, etcd, kube-controller-manager, kube-scheduler, kube-proxy, flannel) all `Running` or `Completed`.

`just cluster-up` exists as an aggregate recipe ordering: `talos-image` → `infra-up` → wait for VMs → `talos-config` → `talos-apply` → wait for secure API → `talos-bootstrap` → `kubeconfig` → wait for nodes Ready.
